**Good Afternoon!** We've got a quick note today from one of our sponsors, [Legion M](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7), offering an investment opportunity in an up and coming Hollywood movie theater you won't want to miss. We hope you find [this opportunity compelling](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7). -- Cole, _CEO_
* * *
[](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7) Emmy-winning founders **Paul Scanlan** and **Jeff Annison** are on a mission to disrupt the $100B movie industry. And they are offering you a chance to get in on the action! Their idea is simple: a studio owned by [**people like you**](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7), who buy the tickets and pay the subscriptions, has competitive advantages. Like harnessing the wisdom of the crowd to make decisions and bringing out the crowd to flood theaters on opening night. [](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7) Their long-term goal is to unite 1 million fans as shareholders (it's what the M̅ in their logo stands for). They believe this could make Legion M one of the most influential companies in Hollywood. Crazy idea, right? Crazy enough, it just might work... Their last movie "My Dead Friend Zoe" stars Morgan Freeman, Ed Harris, Natalie Morales, and Sonequa Martin-Green. At 95% on Rotten Tomatoes, it's one of the best-reviewed films of 2025 - [**and one of the best in Morgan Freeman 's career!**](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7) ****[](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyskL3unDAQxJ_GdCB_YGMKF5FOV-XSprQMXmAjfyDYu7_u7SMuUZ4g7cxod-b38-EfAROW9UfI4GipPkLEORBEX-DrTEAEh0_hpDmk9K7PY95CmYHqCoTFSy51y03Llf8OK9byuEH0ooluAZCgGnBi6O04aKFkszk-TTANo7Y8TAO3OhodjVxGK2XQhssG3XWSG66ElVqJzlpl4jJqHZWcBh1YzyEi1aP7VbHQBkt61xcc3Vxzk9xGtJ9MfWPyzuQdywtO6tKnWr4il67uT8r-vMYAUzfIAROT5hIzRHxmpm57wNju4aACx7nh_tefQ94DruWT-GcKy1s1Wv8HhNBm4FKbXqjmcHH_wpTwNAPr-Xq9-jSl_0n75eTvAAAA__9q65j7) Think a fan-owned company can't make good movies? Think again! That's not all! In just the last couple months, the studio has:
* Closed a 7-figure international rights deal with Universal Studios

* Paid out over $1M in royalties to investors in their movies

* Earned 1.5X last year's total revenue - even though it's only May!

We've got a quick note today from one of our sponsors, , offering an investment opportunity in an up and coming Hollywood movie theater you won't want to miss. We hope you find . -- Cole, CEO Emmy-winning founders Paul Scanlan and Jeff Annison are on a mission to disrupt the $100B movie industry. And they are offering you a chance to get in on the action! Their idea is simple: a studio owned by , who buy the tickets and pay the subscriptions, has competitive advantages. Like harnessing the wisdom of the crowd to make decisions and bringing out the crowd to flood theaters on opening night. Crazy idea, right? Crazy enough, it just might work... Their last movie "My Dead Friend Zoe" stars Morgan Freeman, Ed Harris, Natalie Morales, and Sonequa Martin-Green. At 95% on Rotten Tomatoes, it's one of the best-reviewed films of 2025 - Think a fan-owned company can't make good movies? Think again! That's not all! In just the last couple months, the studio has: Closed a 7-figure international rights deal with Universal Studios Paid out over $1M in royalties to investors in their movies Earned 1.5X last year's total revenue - even though it's only May! - plus a billion-dollar producer, award-winning stars, and top execs from Netflix, Lucasfilm, Disney, Amazon, and Legendary - Legion M's moonshot is well on its way. body:
We need your help to make Virginia a firewall against Trump’s eventual candidate in Virginia.

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108913365/549407929/-581098138?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Scenarios_250603_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2Q0ZGI4Mi1hODQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fjG7XwAJwQyCz_2yZZb2cuAONWFC6bP0b35XImIgnhs=) Derek,

We know you don’t want to worry about losing an incumbent Democrat like Mark in the Senate.

And we wish we could tell you not to worry about it either. Because historically speaking, the party in the White House loses ground in the midterm elections.

But here’s the problem: Donald Trump continues to defy political odds. While the House saw a blue wave in 2018 during Trump’s first term — Republicans in the Senate managed to NET a seat.

Not to mention, the past couple of elections have been really tough on incumbent Democrats. Bob Casey in Pennsylvania? Jon Tester in Montana? Sherrod Brown in Ohio?

**Republicans are looking at the data, and see that Trump only lost Virginia by single-digit percentage points last year. With a strong opponent and a 50 million dollar super PAC, Republicans could win Virginia.**

Look, there’s a lot that can happen in 17 months – but the reality is, we need your help to make Virginia a firewall against Trump’s eventual candidate in Virginia.

name: Nellie Pou
subject: A quick thanks
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pout-header-3.png]

Team,

As we head into the summer months, I just want to take a moment to say thank you.

Thank you for standing with me through the noise, through the attacks, and through every moment of the first few months on the job. It means the world to me and my team.

We're building something special together. A campaign rooted in community, decency, and doing what's right — even if that's not always easy.

While I'll be spending some more time in the district this summer listening to folks throughout NJ-09 and fighting for hard-working families, I know Mike Johnson and his dark money groups will continue spreading lies and tarnishing my record.

I’m not going anywhere.

June is an end-of-quarter month where we have to report our finances to the FEC, so we'll be reaching out quite a bit with updates on where we stand, and I really hope that we can count on you to end this quarter strong.

If you want to get a head start, here's a link: http://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=250603_np_thanks_l1 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=250603_np_thanks_l1]

With you in this fight,

Rep. Nellie Pou (NJ-09)
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pou_headshot_crop.jpg]

About Nellie Pou
Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=b4fc88b1-ac40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=b4fc88b1-ac40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b]
Follow Nellie
https://x.com/NelliePou [https://x.com/NelliePou] https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/ [https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/] https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/ [https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/]

name: Chrissy
subject: [PETITION] NO military parade for Trump’s birthday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
Add your name before midnight!
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108915181/549414178/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTk3YmFmNy1hYzQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RKva7p26gX-nGZMH1J04dTz2WjLtIBATp9nnka5Lb4k=&emci=9c045ecd-fc3f-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=1597baf7-ac40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=21278485)
---
Derek: Every year, on June 14, veterans and servicemembers across the country raise a glass to the U.S. Army on its birthday. Fun fact? The U.S. Army is celebrating its 250th birthday, making it one year older than the United States.
What’s the opposite of a fun fact? President Trump, in true Donald Trump fashion, is making this day about himself. His birthday is also on the 14th so he’s ordered a military parade from the Pentagon, running past the White House lawn, which will cost more than one hundred million dollars ... while at the same time is firing thousands of veterans across the government and slashing services for housing and healthcare that they’ve earned.
Not to mention selecting the deeply unqualified Pete Hegseth as his Secretary of Defense, someone who has recklessly and repeatedly put the lives of servicemembers at risk and undermined our own national security.
So let’s be clear. **This parade _cannot_ be both a celebration of Donald Trump's actions that directly harm our troops and veterans AND a celebration of our Army's service. **
That's why I'm saying "NO" to a military parade for President Trump's birthday – and I'm counting on you to join me, Derek. During this critical time, we need a surge of grassroots supporters like you to make your voice heard in support of our troops. **Will you add your name to our petition and say "NO" to a military parade for Trump's birthday?**

clean_body: Add your name before midnight! | | | Derek: Every year, on June 14, veterans and servicemembers across the country raise a glass to the U.S. Army on its birthday. Fun fact? The U.S. Army is celebrating its 250th birthday, making it one year older than the United States. What’s the opposite of a fun fact? President Trump, in true Donald Trump fashion, is making this day about himself. His birthday is also on the 14th so he’s ordered a military parade from the Pentagon, running past the White House lawn, which will cost more than one hundred million dollars ... while at the same time is firing thousands of veterans across the government and slashing services for housing and healthcare that they’ve earned. Not to mention selecting the deeply unqualified Pete Hegseth as his Secretary of Defense, someone who has recklessly and repeatedly put the lives of servicemembers at risk and undermined our own national security. So let’s be clear. This parade cannot be both a celebration of Donald Trump's actions that directly harm our troops and veterans AND a celebration of our Army's service. That's why I'm saying "NO" to a military parade for President Trump's birthday – and I'm counting on you to join me, Derek. name: Mr. Andriy Borysovych
subject: Greetings from Ukraine,
body:
Greetings from Ukraine,

Mr. Yermak Andriy Borysovych, an entrepreneur businessman from Odessa
Ukraine. Within two years plus, more than 15.2 million people around
the cities of my country Ukraine have been evacuated to a safe
location and out of the country, most especially children with their
parents, nursing mothers and pregnant women, and those who have been
seriously wounded and need urgent medical attention. I was among those
that were able to evacuate to our neighbouring countries and I’m now
in the refugee camp of Ter Apel Groningen in the Netherlands.

I need a foreign partner to enable me to transport my investment
capital and then relocate with my family, honestly i wish I will
discuss more and get along. I need a partner because my investment
capital is in my international account. I’m interested in buying
properties, houses, building real estates, my capital for investment
is ($30 Million USD) . The financial institutions in my country
Ukraine are all shot down due to the crisis of this war on Ukraine
soil by the Russian forces. Meanwhile, if there is any profitable
investment that you have so much experience in your country, then we
can join together as partners since I’m a foreigner.

I came across your e-mail contact through private search while in need
of your assistance and I decided to contact you directly to ask you if
you know any lucrative business investment in your country i can
invest my money since my country Ukraine security and economic
independent has lost to the greatest lower level, and our culture has
lost including our happiness has been taken away from us.

If you are capable of handling this business partnership, contact me
for more details, I will appreciate it if you can contact me
immediately. You may as well tell me a little more about yourself.
Contact me urgently to enable us to proceed with the business. I will
be waiting for your response. My sincere apologies for the
inconvenience.

Thank you!

Mr. Yermak Andriy Borysovych.

name: Stop Peter Thiel via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Stop Trump's "favorite billionaire-tech donor"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis,

Donald Trump is handing his billionaire mega-donor, Peter Thiel, a massive
contract to spy on Americans — using our money. It’s a sinister scheme
that threatens every single person in the country, and sends even more of
our tax dollars to another Trump Oligarch.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31161?source=email-20250603&t=1011&akid=8648%2E5885482%2EHtM_KY ]Headline: Trump Taps Palantir to Compile Data on Americans

We need Congress to immediately HALT Trump's dangerous deal with Thiel’s
Palantir Technologies, a private data-mining company notorious for
intrusive surveillance and privacy violations.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31162?source=email-20250603&t=1012&akid=8648%2E5885482%2EHtM_KY ]SIGN THE PETITION: Tell Congress to stop Trump and Thiel's dangerous
money-making surveillance scheme NOW!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31162?source=email-20250603&t=1013&akid=8648%2E5885482%2EHtM_KY ]ADD YOUR NAME

Palantir is already infamous for targeting immigrants, activists, and
vulnerable communities. Now, Trump is planning to expand Palantir's reach,
granting Thiel’s company unprecedented access to Americans' personal data,
spanning health records, financial transactions, and social media
interactions.

Thiel, Trump’s "favorite billionaire-tech donor," is set to profit
massively from this disturbing deal. Thiel poured millions into Trump's
campaigns and is now cashing in with a multi-billion-dollar government
contract that treats Americans' private lives as a product to be harvested
for profit. Trump is handing this anti-democracy billionaire the keys to
your most personal information, giving Palantir unchecked power over our
personal information.

This isn't democracy — this is Oligarchy, plain and simple. And it’s also
unprecedented.

The New York Times estimates this contract could involve the personal data
of more than 200 million Americans, making Palantir the single largest
repository of private citizen information in U.S. history. Trump and Thiel
aren't just spying — they're selling out our fundamental freedoms to the
highest bidder.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31162?source=email-20250603&t=1014&akid=8648%2E5885482%2EHtM_KY ]Sign the petition: Tell Congress not one penny of public money should
go to enrich Trump's billionaire cronies and fuel the mass surveillance of
Americans! We must stop Trump's Oligarchy takeover.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31162?source=email-20250603&t=1015&akid=8648%2E5885482%2EHtM_KY ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

name: Frank Wojtash: Political Director - Rob for PA
subject: Rob's NEPA Rundown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rob for PA]()
body:
Rob back in NEPA Last Week

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/da64e3b817a3/robs-nepa-rundown?e=131e83a3c2)
---
| | | | |
---
| Good Morning,
Rob was home for the week from D.C. and was able to be out in NEPA again. Rob loves coming home and spending time with the people that he works for. Rob also got his Simplifying Forms for Veterans Claims Act through the House pretty easily and it is on to the Senate. He loves our vets and will always put them first. We also launched the BresnaVan! If you can’t make it to our official offices, you can request our official team to come directly to you!
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| Rob’s week back was jam-packed. He went to community events and visited several businesses and infrastructure that keep NEPA running. He went to the Jam for Jamie benefit, and talked to local Agricultural Leaders to see what he can do on the Ag Committee to put NEPA at the forefront of farming!
---
|
---
[|
---](https://robforpa.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4aaa9665caa590d8638bb435d&id=349f35696a&e=131e83a3c2)
| Our Small Business of the Week is Valley Pest Control, in Plains. Be sure to support local businesses!
---
|
---
|
The Democrats are already coming after our Congressman for 2026! They’re gearing up to lie and slander Rob, and we need your help to put our side of the story out there. If you are able, please donate to our campaign to keep NEPA out of the grasp of the far-left.
You can donate online. Please give any amount you are able, every penny counts! Click the logo below to donate now!
---

---
| Best Regards,Frank WojtashPolitical Director

name: Dan Goldman
subject: NY House Republicans fold again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, _Republicans hold the House majority by three votes. Yet, instead of addressing the pressing issues affecting their constituents, New York Republicans yet again chose to stand with President Trump’s billionaire-first agenda. _ Nearly every single House Republican voted in favor of Donald Trump’s disastrous budget, moving it along to the Senate in the dead of night while most Americans were sleeping. Their proposed budget would rip away health care from 14 million Americans and slash food benefits, child care, public education, and other essential services millions of New Yorkers rely on. **The first few months of Trump’s second term make it clear: the GOP’s values support billionaires and hurt working Americans. And that includes the GOP’s representatives from New York.** They don’t care about lowering costs for working families. They don’t care about protecting Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, or so many other vital government programs Americans depend on. All they care about is falling in line with Trump and helping their special interests. **[We need to make sure voters across the country know who is cutting their health care and their children’s futures — and who is fighting to make life better for them. clean_body: | Peter, Republicans hold the House majority by three votes. Yet, instead of addressing the pressing issues affecting their constituents, New York Republicans yet again chose to stand with President Trump’s billionaire-first agenda. Nearly every single House Republican voted in favor of Donald Trump’s disastrous budget, moving it along to the Senate in the dead of night while most Americans were sleeping. Their proposed budget would rip away health care from 14 million Americans and slash food benefits, child care, public education, and other essential services millions of New Yorkers rely on. The first few months of Trump’s second term make it clear: the GOP’s values support billionaires and hurt working Americans. And that includes the GOP’s representatives from New York. They don’t care about lowering costs for working families. They don’t care about protecting Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, or so many other vital government programs Americans depend on. All they care about is falling in line with Trump and helping their special interests. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you, Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

name: Tina Kotek
subject: We need to protect our industries against tariffs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Big news, Peter — USDA has announced a $16 million purchase of Oregon Pink Shrimp! 🍤 Why is that so huge? We’ve secured a massive investment in our state’s seafood industry and protected the jobs of multi-generational fishing families, who are facing uncertainty and instability due to President Trump’s tariffs. This investment will help our local economies grow and families make ends meet, and insulate our local businesses from the unpredictability of Trump’s tariffs. Almost everyone agrees: these tariffs could have devastating and long-lasting impacts on our economy. I’m going to keep working to ensure that our businesses survive in the face of this instability. **[Add your name right now to stand with me against Trump’s attacks on our local businesses:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108916944/549419935/2006648369?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjRkMjk2NC1iMTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B50rhEeoXIjwiJoxS2kw25yW65P8yd7XuLPz_T6bclM=&emci=564d2964-b140-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=5b4d2964-b140-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15353729)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108916945/549419937/2006648369?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjRkMjk2NC1iMTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B50rhEeoXIjwiJoxS2kw25yW65P8yd7XuLPz_T6bclM=&emci=564d2964-b140-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=5b4d2964-b140-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15353729)
---
Oregon is known for many great things — including excellent food, coffee, agriculture, wine, and timber. These industries fuel local businesses, support manufacturing jobs here in the state, and provide Oregon-made goods around the world.
Situated on the coast, we’re one of the most trade-dependent states in the country. Our ports do billions of dollars worth of business each year, and support local jobs in the area. These tariffs could have disastrous effects on our businesses and consumers and increase costs of living around the state when they’re already too high as it is.
**President Trump’s tariffs would make everything more expensive.** At a time when people are struggling, we need the President to be focused on making lives better, not worse.
That’s what I’m doing, and I’m glad to have you on my side.
Thanks,
Tina
| Tina Kotek is working hard to make Oregon a better place to live and work for all Oregonians – and making real progress on issues like housing and homelessness, education and child care, and mental health and addiction services. **Can you chip in any amount now to support her work?** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108916946/549419940/559856784?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjRkMjk2NC1iMTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B50rhEeoXIjwiJoxS2kw25yW65P8yd7XuLPz_T6bclM=)

These tariffs could have disastrous effects on our businesses and consumers and increase costs of living around the state when they’re already too high as it is. President Trump’s tariffs would make everything more expensive. At a time when people are struggling, we need the President to be focused on making lives better, not worse. That’s what I’m doing, and I’m glad to have you on my side. Thanks, Tina | Tina Kotek is working hard to make Oregon a better place to live and work for all Oregonians – and making real progress on issues like housing and homelessness, education and child care, and mental health and addiction services. Can you chip in any amount now to support her work? | = Tina for Oregon PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 | mailto:info@tinafororegon.com | Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek

body:
We’re just going to remind you of this: America hates dictators. America fights dictators. And America defeats dictators.
#
Peter — here are some guys who really loved a big parade in their honor.

[](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-june25.e5pwgQ&refcode=em250603-1-120&t=2&akid=34433%2E3013755%2EuEadTS)

And the biggest parade guy of them all. Adolf Hitler.
Anybody could make comparisons between these jerks and somebody else you’re probably thinking of. But that’s not what we’re going to do (not today, at least).

Peter,
Under the starry skies of Monte Rio, owls hoot and badgers burrow among the shadows of redwood giants. Now you can keep a little slice of these inspiring California redwoods with you all the time - with **our new moonlit Monte Rio Redwoods sticker!**
Claim your free starry sticker to show your love for the redwoods. It’s perfect for your water bottle, window, laptop or anywhere you like. **[Just click here to claim your sticker now!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108917447/549421616/-1352276412?contactdata=3yXaXNc5TA6pQ4pXf3DaJN2qTZlva94k2g7OCTEkj/AgDK8h1VT8FGsj+hNO7n40BFOjd5+BstZJgmdqTlePS2sVyMYhobxcxBqGUdtTQNLVjeeHVanIfmX487FhJtbZrQh5QJnDV9/S3VsZw1eP2P/2gQgQoxjikYV38ewpEZw=&ms=MonteRio6_Link1&sourceid=1166936&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjcxZTFmN2UxLWIzNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Q4fidA8Xupp9JDb3BGJdjpXxFacQcdGxkUy9I9elEv0=&emci=de5675db-b340-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=71e1f7e1-b340-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=1504551)**

Our design was inspired by the iconic vintage sign that welcomes visitors to the forested hills of Monte Rio, along with a couple of nocturnal critters that call these lands home. It’s our tribute to the nature and beauty of this place that has captured and inspired so many over the years.

clean_body: Check out these nocturnal vibes Peter, Under the starry skies of Monte Rio, owls hoot and badgers burrow among the shadows of redwood giants. Now you can keep a little slice of these inspiring California redwoods with you all the time - with our new moonlit Monte Rio Redwoods sticker! Claim your free starry sticker to show your love for the redwoods. It’s perfect for your water bottle, window, laptop or anywhere you like. Our design was inspired by the iconic vintage sign that welcomes visitors to the forested hills of Monte Rio, along with a couple of nocturnal critters that call these lands home. It’s our tribute to the nature and beauty of this place that has captured and inspired so many over the years. These forests connect over 22,000 acres of vital habitat, shelter endangered species, and strengthen resilience against climate change, wildfire, and drought. Now more than ever, safeguarding these ecosystems is crucial for biodiversity and planetary health. With gratitude, Save the Redwoods League | | | | ---|---|---|--- Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States |

Hey Peter, We’re nearly halfway through the year — and that means we’re already a _quarter_ of the way through this election cycle. Time is flying. That’s why I want to take a moment to thank you. Every bit of support you gave during our end-of-month push has helped us stay on track. But I’ve got to be honest with you: we can’t afford to take our foot off the gas. Our first official **end-of-quarter fundraising deadline** of the campaign is just a few weeks away. Once it passes, we’ll have to report our fundraising numbers publicly — and you better believe establishment Republicans will be watching closely. They’re already banding together to defend Thom Tillis and hold this seat. They see beating us as the key to keeping their grip on Congress. We see this fight as a chance to prove that the people still come first. Let’s show them the movement we’re building. [**Can you chip in anything ahead of this critical EOQ deadline? clean_body: | = Hey Peter, We’re nearly halfway through the year — and that means we’re already a quarter of the way through this election cycle. Time is flying. That’s why I want to take a moment to thank you. Every bit of support you gave during our end-of-month push has helped us stay on track. But I’ve got to be honest with you: we can’t afford to take our foot off the gas. Our first official end-of-quarter fundraising deadline of the campaign is just a few weeks away. Once it passes, we’ll have to report our fundraising numbers publicly — and you better believe establishment Republicans will be watching closely. They’re already banding together to defend Thom Tillis and hold this seat. They see beating us as the key to keeping their grip on Congress. We see this fight as a chance to prove that the people still come first. Let’s show them the movement we’re building. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue, your donation to Wiley Nickel will process immediately: ** | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for being part of this, Wiley Wiley Nickel for North Carolina PO Box 563 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States ** | mailto:info@wileynickel.com | Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
|
---
Hi Peter, I wanted to show you something: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108918834/549426338/-2009428703?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTY3MjU4ZS1iNjQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iSET2v_W8yNrCU7aOvcbfyJdTg61PHlAhKCUhchI2Z8=) This is me on my red eye flight from Phoenix back to DC this weekend. While I was back home in Arizona, I held a town hall to listen to Arizonans’ concerns about Republican cuts to health care and education, attacks on immigrants and trans youth, and so much more. I also conducted a surprise oversight visit to the Eloy Detention Center in Arizona, an immigration detention facility where I heard about inhumane conditions. One thing is very clear: people across Arizona are being impacted by this administration’s actions every single day, and they deserve representatives in Congress who will fight for them. Now that I’m back in DC, I’m ready to get back to work for people like you, Peter. **But I can’t do it alone. If you’d like to chip in any amount to help me continue fighting for Arizonans in Congress, you can use this link to do so:** **_**[http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ya-em-jun25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108918835/549426339/-2009428703?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTY3MjU4ZS1iNjQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iSET2v_W8yNrCU7aOvcbfyJdTg61PHlAhKCUhchI2Z8=)**_** Thanks for everything you do, Yass

clean_body: | Hi Peter, I wanted to show you something: = This is me on my red eye flight from Phoenix back to DC this weekend. While I was back home in Arizona, I held a town hall to listen to Arizonans’ concerns about Republican cuts to health care and education, attacks on immigrants and trans youth, and so much more. I also conducted a surprise oversight visit to the Eloy Detention Center in Arizona, an immigration detention facility where I heard about inhumane conditions. One thing is very clear: people across Arizona are being impacted by this administration’s actions every single day, and they deserve representatives in Congress who will fight for them. Now that I’m back in DC, I’m ready to get back to work for people like you, Peter. But I can’t do it alone. If you’d like to chip in any amount to help me continue fighting for Arizonans in Congress, you can use this link to do so: = Thanks for everything you do, Yass Yassamin Ansari for Congress PO Box 13524 Phoenix, AZ 85002 United States | mailto:info@yassaminforcongress.com | Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress.

United States Supreme Court challenging the decision by the U.S. Court
of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit in the case filed on behalf of
Congressman Mike Bost and two presidential electors from Illinois to
prevent state election officials from extending Election Day for 14
days beyond the date established by federal law.

In fact, it's an announcement that could put the entire free world back on track...

# [Xi Jinping Could Save America With One Simple Announcement](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=165115882&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTE1ODgyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODE5NDQsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU3Mzk0NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XF9eOB6_lDe2ItRe6DQ3IK7zDvDF-rJi6XrF1Dzby8g)
### In fact, it's an announcement that could put the entire free world back on track...
| | [Matt Robison](https://substack.com/@mattrobison2)
---
| Jun 3| | | ∙| | Guest post
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@mattrobison2)
---

---

**[by Matt Robison](https://substack.com/redirect/516eb80f-9161-422e-b0ee-54cad9a34c3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/06db2afa-71e7-4a7b-9577-48a4e5b0fd67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
`Independent Media For $1.32/Week!`
_**We 're running a special! *Full Access* to Blue Amp for only $1.32 per week!**_
_**Please support our work covering stories monopoly media ignores. And amplifying indy media to create a viable, powerful, media ecosystem independent of corporate interests!**_
_**Amplify Good. Support Indy Media!**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.cdxwAUXVk8f_4Nf5Uyvobes0zrNaqDbjtI9Qv4LfX0s?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165115882)
When Pope Leo XIV ascended to the loggia of St. Peter's Basilica two weeks ago, he was bathed in the shadow of a long-gone empire. The throne of St. Peter is in Rome because that was the center of the world in the time of Jesus; the Church speaks the language of a civilization that peaked two millennia ago.
Echoes of the ancient Romans still whisper to all of us. Our laws are filled with Roman ideas. Our calendars were designed by Romans, the days and months named after their gods and emperors. Our architecture, measurements, and government are Roman-infused hand-me-downs. If you want an idea to sound more sophisticated, you talk about it in Latin--that's what our doctors, lawyers, and scientists do.
All of which serves as a reminder that a mighty fallen civilization can have a long afterglow. And that should be some comfort to America as we slouch into our age of decline.
Alaric may not be at the gates, but the signs of decay are hard to ignore. Americans have become substantially less educated and pay much less attention to the world. We elect [parasitic leaders who feed us facile "member berries" about restoring our former "greatness" while actually wrecking](https://substack.com/redirect/fef530e5-8407-43e4-bfb6-8b2cda0d2a88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) our credit, underminin[g](https://substack.com/redirect/9d4a0078-9e78-4949-a0be-e6c98640fdb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the scientific research that forms the foundation of our prosperity, and dragging down our democratic freedoms.
They send the world's investment fleeing pell-mell away from us, and are sapping our very brains. We've become anti-social, unhappy, and cantankerous, and like anyone struggling through a depression, we've fallen too far down to seemingly do much about it.
So there's a little consolation in the prospect that the afterglow of American economic, intellectual, and military power from the last century could keep our descendants relatively well off for generations to come, the way the eastern Roman empire persisted for 1,000 years after the sputtering decline that culminated in the sack of Rome.
But what if we don't want our grandkids merely to coast on the dynamism of our grandparents, aimless scions of an old fortune? History suggests that there may be a way to spur us to start creating new fortunes again: face an existential threat.
Of course, that didn't work for the Romans, whose internal rot had progressed too far. But it did work for the ancient Greek city states that preceded them. The threat posed by the Persian empire forced them into rapid growth and evolution.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c542ecc8-6dae-4e0d-bb83-216df53ee095?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In the case of Athens, this threat led to the invention of democracy, flourishing commerce, and a flowering of literature, philosophy, and science--innovations that passed to the Romans, and thence to us. Facing a deadly enemy can really work; in this case, creating a powerful enough impetus to continue shaping societies 2,500 years later.
Still, while threats can be great motivators, we don't exactly want to get into an actual shooting war. What about a proxy, then? In the same way that you can substitute a vaccine for a living, deadly virus in order to kick your immune system into gear, could we provoke a robust national response by getting challenged to a different kind of world competition?
What if we could get into another space race?
From the Soviet Union's 1957 Sputnik launch through America's 1969 moon landing, the space race was an unmitigated boon for the United States. This gladiatorial competition generated literally incalculable economic growth and improvements in our well-being.
`Independent Media For $1.32/Week!`
_**We 're running a special! *Full Access* to Blue Amp for only $1.32 per week!**_
_**Please support our work covering stories monopoly media ignores. And amplifying indy media to create a viable, powerful, media ecosystem independent of corporate interests!**_
_**Amplify Good. Support Indy Media!**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.cdxwAUXVk8f_4Nf5Uyvobes0zrNaqDbjtI9Qv4LfX0s?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165115882)
It gave us basically the entire computing and information technology sector, plus all kinds of products that we buy, sell, and use to this day, such as--and this is just a short list of examples--sneakers, LED lights, home insulation, camera phones, smoke detectors, and artificial limbs. It gave us national purpose. It renewed our pride.
Of course, even if such a competition is just what America needs, we can't launch a race without a competitor. And let's face it--we're unlikely to do it under President Donald Trump's reckless scientific destruction. We need a foil, a real rival to jolt us into action.
That's where Xi Jinping could solve our problems in a stroke. What would happen if he announced that China would be the first nation to land a human on Mars, and would do so in the next ten years?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4ab72cdc-a862-4abe-8b34-78a0c438a573?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Would Trump respond? You better believe it. Nothing riles him up like losing to China and looking like a chump, especially since he's already publicly committed to being the first to get to Mars.
And once a few tech titans explain to him the mind-boggling fortunes theoretically available in space mining, that the race to Mars is really a race to develop the capabilities to start exploiting asteroid wealth, and that a single asteroid (Psyche) has an estimated value of $100 quintillion--orders of magnitude more than the entire global economy--do you think this highly transactional, avaricious president might start to get interested?
Would American industry respond? See above. Not to mention the lure of the hundreds of billions in federal contracts that would become available once the U.S. government got into it.
Would the American public respond? Very likely. Two-thirds of Americans, including 80 percent of Republicans, already support maintaining America's lead over China in artificial intelligence. Imagine how they would feel about losing the biggest technical competition in history.
And would it work? Would America get a new infusion of innovation, focus, and economic vitality? Considering the research that would be required for the myriad of next-generation telecommunications and computing equipment, materials science, botany, electricity generation and storage that would all be supercharged by a second space race, it's hard to see how it wouldn't pay off in very visible ways, and pay off very quickly.
And by the way, it's also hard to see how China wouldn't gain just as much, especially given its growing economic challenges and desire to, accordin[g](https://substack.com/redirect/ac210b59-d0fb-41e7-9782-64e40772aaf4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the Congressional Research Service, "boost growth and productivity by investing in innovation, education, digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, and emerging technologies." Sound like a prescription for a space race?
In the brilliant streaming series _For All Mankind_ , Hollywood made a compelling case that the entire world would have been better off if the Soviets had beaten the U.S. to the moon in 1969 and given us the motivation to keep the space rivalry going.
We can never know. But we can be pretty confident that in 2025, that kind of kick is exactly what we need, what China needs, and what the world needs. Your move, President Xi.
* * *
_This is a guest post from Blue Amp contributor Matt Robison--former Democratic Congressional chief of staff, campaign manager, and[Newsweek columnist](https://substack.com/redirect/6d0c752e-375a-437d-b470-bf3ad5842bff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). **Subscribe** to Matt's Substack, [Worth Knowing](https://substack.com/redirect/516eb80f-9161-422e-b0ee-54cad9a34c3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), to support and amplify indy media and a Blue Amp contributor! _

This is a guest post from Blue Amp contributor Matt Robison--former Democratic Congressional chief of staff, campaign manager, and. Senate Republicans have made it clear that they’re more interested in giving Donald Trump a blank check than standing up for everyday Americans.
They’ve rubber-stamped his extreme judges, ignored attacks on our democracy, and stood by as he’s tried to dismantle Social Security, Medicare, and environmental protections.
But we don’t have to accept this — we can lay the groundwork it will take to win critical Senate seats on the ballot, defend Democratic-held seats, and flip the Senate blue.
That means supporting Democratic candidates as they take on wealthy or well-connected opponents. That means showing up in high-stakes races to ensure voters know who’s really fighting for them in Washington. Most importantly, that means preparing for the fights ahead _now_.
Republicans are going to flood these races with special interest money to keep their majority. I know if we stand together, we can fight back and win.

On May 20th, Donald Trump told reporters the following about his "Big, Beautiful Bill": **"We’re not changing Medicaid, only cutting waste, fraud and abuse."** But that was an outright lie. ❌ The budget bill passed by Republican House members does change Medicaid — **and it’s far worse than what he claimed.** This bill cuts reimbursements to states that cover people’s health care, forces Medicaid recipients to work 80 hours a month and bans payments to nonprofits that provide abortions. This isn’t about _"cutting waste"_ — it’s pushing a political agenda that will hurt millions across the country, especially our communities in NM-02. Gabe is working hard to fight against these cuts, but we need your help to keep going. Every dollar you give helps us push back against this attack on our health care. [No matter the size, your support makes a difference — whether it’s $10, $25, or $50. Please chip in today so Gabe can keep standing up for southern New Mexico and the members of our communities who depend on Medicaid.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108919721/549429459/1448278484?amount=10&source=SBD_GV_EM_FR_060325_BudgetBill_60DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWQxNTFjNi1iOTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tbKZbY-10GGPlf32NeyqG-mC44qe3tzXoaMWciYCfT4=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | On May 20th, Donald Trump told reporters the following about his "Big, Beautiful Bill": "We’re not changing Medicaid, only cutting waste, fraud and abuse." But that was an outright lie. ❌ The budget bill passed by Republican House members does change Medicaid — and it’s far worse than what he claimed. This bill cuts reimbursements to states that cover people’s health care, forces Medicaid recipients to work 80 hours a month and bans payments to nonprofits that provide abortions. This isn’t about "cutting waste" — it’s pushing a political agenda that will hurt millions across the country, especially our communities in NM-02. Gabe is working hard to fight against these cuts, but we need your help to keep going. name: Team Marie
subject: Will you help us surprise Marie?
body:
| | Peter, tomorrow is a big day: it’s Marie’s birthday!

Marie works hard in Congress every day to deliver results for Southwest Washington. Since day one, she has remained committed to lowering costs for working families, defending democracy, protecting reproductive rights, and keeping our communities safe.

[We want to thank Marie for all she does, and remind her just how many people are standing with her as she faces another tough re-election fight this year. Add your name to her card here, and let’s make this birthday extra special! →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108919981/549430269/1140984919?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_250603_1_1&refcode2=390653f0-ba40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTA2NTNmMC1iYTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KZQz0rldODXOl4dpOPa8zRFBVzCpW0kDqfZE-UUvKZM=&emci=63abbda0-b940-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=390653f0-ba40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=31138575)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108919984/549430271/1140984919?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_250603_1_1&refcode2=390653f0-ba40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTA2NTNmMC1iYTQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KZQz0rldODXOl4dpOPa8zRFBVzCpW0kDqfZE-UUvKZM=&emci=63abbda0-b940-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=390653f0-ba40-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=31138575)
We’re so proud to have Marie’s leadership in Congress. She’s working across the aisle to get things done for our rural communities and working families – and she’s not slowing down now.

Let’s show Marie our appreciation for her tireless work and make this birthday one to remember.

When we talk about families struggling to make ends meet, we mean it. Take a look at this fact: **daycare costs in the United States have gone up to a staggering $28,000 a year on average — that’s about 35% of a household’s annual income.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108920023/549430455/-1931564815?amount=&refcode=AU_FSP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250603_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTk2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNmFlOGUxOC1iYjQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BXLqkoTIKD_t3NLAh0FEHHMfWQde5i7-hBx59z9YDHY=) With the skyrocketing costs of gas, groceries, health care, and other daily essentials on top of it, the cost of raising and growing a family is becoming unsustainable for too many working parents. **And while the GOP claims to be the “pro-life” party, Republicans just voted to cut food assistance for 7 million children nationwide — making it _that much harder_ for working families to get by.** Republicans’ agenda isn’t just misguided, it's cruel. subject: Trump's budget cuts are jeopardizing abortion access
body:
Urgent news out of the Midwest |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108920478/549432124/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE604_20250603_ClinicsClosing_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056311&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDg1NGIyZi1iZDQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b0fV0eQP09xq_fWFUVFql5fDiKzhttMKKQV_bOhikwY=) Peter,

**Republicans' latest budget cuts are jeopardizing abortion access even in blue states.**

The Trump administration has started to withhold millions in federal funding for essential health care services, like birth control, cervical cancer screenings, and testing for sexually transmitted diseases — and House Republicans just passed a budget that would bar Planned Parenthood from accepting Medicaid. **That's right — Republicans want to make it harder for lower-income patients to get the care they need.**

As a result, in the past month alone, _Planned Parenthood has been forced to close 12 clinics_ across Minnesota, Michigan, and Iowa, cutting dozens of jobs and making access to reproductive health care dramatically harder for thousands of people. This is a wake-up call: even in "blue states," our fundamental freedoms are under direct attack by anti-choice Republicans.

****[**Stand up against the cruelty of anti-choice extremism and support Democratic pro-choice women who will defend our access to reproductive care in EVERY state:**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108920479/549432125/-1082512980?refcode=WUEFE604_20250603_ClinicsClosing_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056311&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDg1NGIyZi1iZDQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b0fV0eQP09xq_fWFUVFql5fDiKzhttMKKQV_bOhikwY=)**** | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108920480/549432126/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE604_20250603_ClinicsClosing_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056311&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDg1NGIyZi1iZDQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b0fV0eQP09xq_fWFUVFql5fDiKzhttMKKQV_bOhikwY=)
---
Planned Parenthood North Central States will now operate just ten brick-and-mortar clinics in all of Minnesota, two in Iowa, two in Nebraska, and _one single clinic_ in South Dakota. Iowa already bans abortion after six weeks – forcing many patients to travel across state lines to get care, so these closures will impact their access even further.

Anti-choice extremists know that coming after Planned Parenthood's funding could shut down sexual and reproductive health providers across the country. They're on a mission to control our bodies and our lives by any means necessary – but we won't let them.

****[**Donate now to fight back by electing pro-choice leaders across the country who will protect our fundamental freedoms:**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108920481/549432127/-1082512980?refcode=WUEFE604_20250603_ClinicsClosing_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056311&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDg1NGIyZi1iZDQwLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b0fV0eQP09xq_fWFUVFql5fDiKzhttMKKQV_bOhikwY=)****

clean_body: Urgent news out of the Midwest | = Peter, Republicans' latest budget cuts are jeopardizing abortion access even in blue states. The Trump administration has started to withhold millions in federal funding for essential health care services, like birth control, cervical cancer screenings, and testing for sexually transmitted diseases — and House Republicans just passed a budget that would bar Planned Parenthood from accepting Medicaid. That's right — Republicans want to make it harder for lower-income patients to get the care they need. As a result, in the past month alone, Planned Parenthood has been forced to close 12 clinics across Minnesota, Michigan, and Iowa, cutting dozens of jobs and making access to reproductive health care dramatically harder for thousands of people. This is a wake-up call: even in "blue states," our fundamental freedoms are under direct attack by anti-choice Republicans. **= | = Planned Parenthood North Central States will now operate just ten brick-and-mortar clinics in all of Minnesota, two in Iowa, two in Nebraska, and one single clinic in South Dakota. Iowa already bans abortion after six weeks – forcing many patients to travel across state lines to get care, so these closures will impact their access even further. Anti-choice extremists know that coming after Planned Parenthood's funding could shut down sexual and reproductive health providers across the country. They're on a mission to control our bodies and our lives by any means necessary – but we won't let them. =** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Emily Parés (she/her/hers) Interim Vice President of State and Local Campaigns, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Sign the Petition (via Left Action)
subject: Stop Trump’s Moronic Trade Wars Before He Wrecks the Economy (Again)
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
## [Stop Trump’s Moronic Trade Wars Before He Wrecks the Economy (Again)](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td8730029-7bb5-4fe3-ae2e-45a451d79d3d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T107d717d-1ee5-4599-bfd5-46f881207e1d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | It's happening again. Donald Trump is on the brink of launching yet another self-inflicted economic crisis — this time with a [massive 50% tariff on steel](https://default.salsalabs.org/T92ca116a-0dc4-4fca-bf4f-5376b7663621/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) and aluminum set to take effect this week. **We've seen this movie before: Trump announces some astronomical, economically insane tariff — usually targeting allies. Targets retaliate. Markets tank. Global trade teeters. American families pay the price.** He usually chickens out, but the long-term damage to trust and cooperation with our most important partners goes on. This latest move is especially dangerous. Steel and aluminum aren't niche materials — they're everywhere: food cans, construction materials, cars, appliances, housing infrastructure. A 50% tariff won't just raise prices — it will ripple through every corner of the economy, hitting working families, small businesses, and supply chains that are still recovering from the last round of chaos. This isn't just bad policy. It's economic sabotage, driven by ego and ignorance. We need to speak out — loud and fast. Americans must show unified, unrelenting opposition to this reckless act. [**Tell Trump: "Stop launching moronic trade wars that hurt the U.S. most of all!"**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T467558a0-655c-44eb-b9f9-b8011ecd7659/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Add your name. Demand accountability. Let's stop Trump before he burns down the economy — again.
---
| | | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T69083646-5268-45f7-ad46-99cec71fb76d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Thank you, Left Action

name: David Shadburn, LCV
subject: [TAKE ACTION]: Tell senators to protect public lands and clean energy incentives
body:
Add your voice to our campaign

If you are unable to see the message below, [click here to view](https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI1NTgxMzgS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjI0MDA0MDMxOQS2&mt=1&rt=0).
| | | |
---
|
* * *
---
| | | | Peter — Recently, House Republicans approved their billionaire tax scam by just a single vote. Their bill would gut clean energy investments, drive up energy costs for consumers, and create pay-to-play schemes for polluters to ignore environmental safeguards and drill on public lands, along with other threats to people’s health and safety. **It’s on track to be one of the most harmful, costly, and sweeping pieces of legislation in recent history.**
---
| | The Republicans’ bill is not yet law — and we need your help urging senators to STOP the worst attacks on the environment or OPPOSE it entirely.
---
| | [**_Senators are negotiating the bill RIGHT NOW. We can’t afford to let them put our economy and our future at risk — tell your senators to oppose ANY attempt to gut incentives for clean energy or roll back protections for our air, lands, and water._**](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/x_yzTgdGpET~)
---
| | Senate Republicans have set a July 4th goal to pass this bill. The Republican majority is razor-thin, and a number of Republican senators have already spoken out against the House’s sledgehammer approach to clean energy. Without their votes, the bill cannot pass into law, but there’s enormous pressure on them to cave to Trump and support it.
---

Their bill would gut clean energy investments, drive up energy costs for consumers, and create pay-to-play schemes for polluters to ignore environmental safeguards and drill on public lands, along with other threats to people’s health and safety. It’s on track to be one of the most harmful, costly, and sweeping pieces of legislation in recent history. | | The Republicans’ bill is not yet law — and we need your help urging senators to STOP the worst attacks on the environment or OPPOSE it entirely. | | | | Senate Republicans have set a July 4th goal to pass this bill. The Republican majority is razor-thin, and a number of Republican senators have already spoken out against the House’s sledgehammer approach to clean energy. name: David Hogg
subject: Thank you, Peter
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I don’t take this milestone lightly.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250603-1&t=1&akid=2216%2E587540%2EIEH6z6)
**Leaders We Deserve just hit a major milestone:_200,000 grassroots donors._**
**That’s 200,000 people who believe in a future led by young, bold, progressive leaders. 200,000 people who believe in _us_ and our movement.**
I don't take this moment lightly, Peter. I’m filled with gratitude – and I want to take a moment to say **THANK YOU.** It may sound like a cliche at this point, but we truly can’t do this work without you. **Everything we do is powered by this team.**
Even though we’re raising millions to elect the next generation of badass progressives, we stretch every dollar. When I’m on the road, I crash on friends’ couches whenever I can. We’re trying to be the best stewards of your donations that we can be — **because you’re not just donors to us, you’re _why_ we do this.**
We’re building a transformational movement. Here’s who’s powering this work:
* More than **11,000** of our donors are teachers.
* **13,000** are retired folks who believe in our vision for the future.
* Thousands more are students, organizers, nurses, community leaders, and working people from every background fighting for the leaders we deserve.

That’s who I’m thinking about when we’re knocking doors in 100-degree heat or staying up until 2AM planning strategy. That’s what keeps me going in this fight.
So if you’ve ever chipped in $5, shared a link, or even just opened one of these emails — thank you. Truly. You all are the backbone of this entire organization.
This work is hard. The odds are steep. But knowing that we have hundreds of thousands of people in this fight with us? **That makes all the difference.** ❤️
**[Peter, if you're ready to stay in the fight, make a $5 contribution now.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250603_1_thankyou?refcode=em20250603-2216&amount=5&refcode2=2216_587540_IEH6z6&akid=2216%2E587540%2EIEH6z6)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250603_1_thankyou?amount=5&refcode=em20250603-2216&refcode2=2216_587540_IEH6z6&akid=2216%2E587540%2EIEH6z6)**
Thank you!!!
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250603-1&t=2&refcode2=2216_587540_IEH6z6&akid=2216%2E587540%2EIEH6z6)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2216%2E587540%2EIEH6z6)

611 Pennsylvania Ave SE

Washington, DC 20003

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/718683d6-129d-4c96-be06-d17f450bd69c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/96c8a98a-274b-4ac8-9280-ca1f6ab50a99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/e3f3de73-32b4-4de1-b141-b7007305999c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Stepping in to 'Lead On,' Daniel Pullin becomes TCU's new chancellor,**" Fort Worth Report's **Shomial Ahmad** - "**Daniel Pullin** , who became TCU's chancellor on June 1, considers himself an "accidental academic." His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one.

He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling.

"I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day," said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. "It's just been an honor and a delight to build this career."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/0f9bef3a-78cc-4ac5-8256-2289a40b493f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Pullin takes the reins of TCU at a time of transition for higher education and college athletics, where [federal directives](https://substack.com/redirect/b558b533-a45f-48a3-b487-6900a7815093?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are changing the landscape of what's offered at colleges around the nation and a pending NCAA settlement [that will change](https://substack.com/redirect/bf6388a8-26df-4b9d-ab6e-a6156b9e002b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) college athletics -- especially at a Big 12 Conference school like TCU.

"It's a position of privilege," Pullin said during an interview with the Report. "(I've tried) to do that with a style of civility and open-mindedness and in a leadership style that embraces others and (tries) to achieve things together and not alone."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/0f9bef3a-78cc-4ac5-8256-2289a40b493f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of **Victor Boschini** , TCU's longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university's chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus.

The university is on an expansive track.

Pullin led the process to devise its [10-year strategic plan](https://substack.com/redirect/8ce51e08-4b08-45fb-a491-07359881af2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "Lead On: Values in Action," with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an "unmatched" student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution.

The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU's academic path.

"Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold," said **James Riddlesperger** , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. "The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin's major challenge will be to transform promise into reality."

The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will "require forward-thinking leadership."

**Rebecca Sharpless** , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan.

"As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics," said Sharpless, a professor in American history.

While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics.

"Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter," said TCU Board of Trustees Chair **Kit Moncrief** in a statement. "Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success."

One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution.

In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU's rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year.

TCU's plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of "very high spending and doctorate production" involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates.

TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin." [FW Report](https://substack.com/redirect/67ce11ab-f40b-433a-9d58-c497ab4c9f60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS "Stepping in to 'Lead On,' Daniel Pullin becomes TCU's new chancellor," Fort Worth Report's Shomial Ahmad - "Daniel Pullin , who became TCU's chancellor on June 1, considers himself an "accidental academic." His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one. He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling. "I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day," said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. "It's just been an honor and a delight to build this career." Pullin takes the reins of TCU at a time of transition for higher education and college athletics, where are changing the landscape of what's offered at colleges around the nation and a pending NCAA settlement college athletics -- especially at a Big 12 Conference school like TCU. "It's a position of privilege," Pullin said during an interview with the Report. "(I've tried) to do that with a style of civility and open-mindedness and in a leadership style that embraces others and (tries) to achieve things together and not alone." In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of Victor Boschini , TCU's longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university's chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus. The university is on an expansive track. Pullin led the process to devise its , "Lead On: Values in Action," with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an "unmatched" student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution. The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU's academic path. "Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold," said James Riddlesperger , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. "The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin's major challenge will be to transform promise into reality." The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will "require forward-thinking leadership." Rebecca Sharpless , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan. "As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics," said Sharpless, a professor in American history. While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics. "Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter," said TCU Board of Trustees Chair Kit Moncrief in a statement. "Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success." One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution. In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU's rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year. TCU's plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of "very high spending and doctorate production" involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates. TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin." * **TOP NEWS**

"**Stepping in to 'Lead On,' Daniel Pullin becomes TCU's new chancellor,**" Fort Worth Report's **Shomial Ahmad** - "**Daniel Pullin** , who became TCU's chancellor on June 1, considers himself an "accidental academic." His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one.

He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling.

"I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day," said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. "It's just been an honor and a delight to build this career."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/9234797a-df33-4054-829f-c07ba9fb9420?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Pullin takes the reins of TCU at a time of transition for higher education and college athletics, where [federal directives](https://substack.com/redirect/be231f67-59f1-4184-b261-e8053a970640?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are changing the landscape of what's offered at colleges around the nation and a pending NCAA settlement [that will change](https://substack.com/redirect/01ba9524-59a5-4602-a1d7-7f958fecb46c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) college athletics -- especially at a Big 12 Conference school like TCU.

"It's a position of privilege," Pullin said during an interview with the Report. "(I've tried) to do that with a style of civility and open-mindedness and in a leadership style that embraces others and (tries) to achieve things together and not alone."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/9234797a-df33-4054-829f-c07ba9fb9420?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of Victor Boschini, TCU's longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university's chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus.

The university is on an expansive track.

Pullin led the process to devise its [10-year strategic plan](https://substack.com/redirect/a9a18c55-53da-45b8-a9fe-efd186d9100a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "Lead On: Values in Action," with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an "unmatched" student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution.

The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU's academic path.

"Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold," said **James Riddlesperger** , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. "The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin's major challenge will be to transform promise into reality."

The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will "require forward-thinking leadership."

**Rebecca Sharpless** , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan.

"As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics," said Sharpless, a professor in American history.

While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics.

"Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter," said TCU Board of Trustees Chair **Kit Moncrief** in a statement. "Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success."

One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution.

In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU's rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year.

TCU's plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of "very high spending and doctorate production" involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates.

TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin."
