TOP NEWS "Stepping in to 'Lead On,' Daniel Pullin becomes TCU's new chancellor," Fort Worth Report's Shomial Ahmad - "Daniel Pullin , who became TCU's chancellor on June 1, considers himself an "accidental academic." His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one. He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling. "I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day," said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. "It's just been an honor and a delight to build this career." Pullin takes the reins of TCU at a time of transition for higher education and college athletics, where are changing the landscape of what's offered at colleges around the nation and a pending NCAA settlement college athletics -- especially at a Big 12 Conference school like TCU. "It's a position of privilege," Pullin said during an interview with the Report. "(I've tried) to do that with a style of civility and open-mindedness and in a leadership style that embraces others and (tries) to achieve things together and not alone." In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of Victor Boschini, TCU's longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university's chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus. The university is on an expansive track. Pullin led the process to devise its , "Lead On: Values in Action," with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an "unmatched" student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution. The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU's academic path. "Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold," said James Riddlesperger , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. "The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin's major challenge will be to transform promise into reality." The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will "require forward-thinking leadership." Rebecca Sharpless , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan. "As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics," said Sharpless, a professor in American history. While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics. "Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter," said TCU Board of Trustees Chair Kit Moncrief in a statement. "Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success." One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution. In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU's rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year. TCU's plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of "very high spending and doctorate production" involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates. TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin." “**Stepping in to ‘Lead On,’ Daniel Pullin becomes TCU’s new chancellor,** ” Fort Worth Report's **Shomial Ahmad** – “**Daniel Pullin** , who became TCU’s chancellor on June 1, considers himself an “accidental academic.” His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one.

He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling.

“I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day,” said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. “It’s just been an honor and a delight to build this career.”

In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of Victor Boschini, TCU’s longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university’s chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus.

The university is on an expansive track.

Pullin led the process to devise its [10-year strategic plan](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.vPsDTRRm1cA9m3Oa8MsMcNPW7q6zFOW4jQi-NdCeP6KcxfOARgjsIlqE2RAm-CABYBMEiz2W6LeF1wKBCBkz-4FVn_cxINcHLXpCpHe-MfGtxdyu-TKL6gYN__oSYVmjFtniP1xnCkQh3DYPE_Nh8cTewboi9j8Kwas_7eM5PycO-uHOGVUU9uh7cFJbweofuq1a5uMA2Lu8fhQTCh4SLRQcZaGrV1fdcI3csq6HBovadyPvS0mvNQMYEQQGVV-iWmL6MrH8hRxsRlflBZYL-AG5JocVK0JJYoKmPvILy2SswRUUyPluXgTCXYEmJOBPT4S17g1SZtDVkyPe59CpC73aDQjs-bpUJL_6OGj1jhKxDKpDY8cJThzCBLm1G7_jj5_PEy07K_ssF-13PEIn1R-FLdWm6lhifx5YU4PdRfAzgDX05b00E0Vfsij0eT4NwX5QKt24BSl6A3LYZ-vXlQhkTm8iFNzVAMjEJ-SC9Kv8tsK0xwOdXPU1mIE1IpLp0t9PkDx57W5DlWikhkewjBYF_Q0QYepFZG2Trs4nPGFglBEuuqbzoe2xG6zV4bvTsAPXk0fh8OaxQ4ugx9SO3eWhyovnfZeC5D6C7jNe1UM/4h1/DQGeVrmWTz6lyYOax3YM9w/h18/h001.Me-s4AuIY7NCwowZtqYqB5gBdDve-Gj7PptlvwVpmS4), “Lead On: Values in Action,” with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an “unmatched” student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution.

The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU’s academic path.

“Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold,” said **James Riddlesperger** , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. “The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin’s major challenge will be to transform promise into reality.”

The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will “require forward-thinking leadership.”

**Rebecca Sharpless** , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan.

“As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics,” said Sharpless, a professor in American history.

While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics.

“Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter,” said TCU Board of Trustees Chair **Kit Moncrief** in a statement. “Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success.”

One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution.

In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU’s rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year.

TCU’s plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of “very high spending and doctorate production” involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates.

TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin." Our concise, 5-minute read lands in your inbox each morning at no cost. Experience news without the noise; let 1440 help you make up your own mind. Sign up now and invite your friends and family to be part of the informed. | TOP NEWS “Stepping in to ‘Lead On,’ Daniel Pullin becomes TCU’s new chancellor, ” Fort Worth Report's Shomial Ahmad – “Daniel Pullin , who became TCU’s chancellor on June 1, considers himself an “accidental academic.” His path to leading a Big 12 university was never a straight one. He went to business school at Harvard University, law school at the University of Oklahoma, worked as a business analyst and started an entrepreneurial program at his alma mater at OU, where he found his calling. “I just fell in love with the classroom and students and seeing the direct impact that you could have in a scaled way, on many people every day,” said Pullin, who came to TCU in 2019 to lead the TCU Neeley School of Business. “It’s just been an honor and a delight to build this career.” » Pullin takes the reins of TCU at a time of transition for higher education and college athletics, where are changing the landscape of what’s offered at colleges around the nation and a pending NCAA settlement college athletics — especially at a Big 12 Conference school like TCU. “It’s a position of privilege,” Pullin said during an interview with the Report. “(I’ve tried) to do that with a style of civility and open-mindedness and in a leadership style that embraces others and (tries) to achieve things together and not alone.” » In 2023, he assumed the position of presidency, where he learned in the footsteps of Victor Boschini, TCU’s longtime chancellor who served for more than two decades. Boschini stepped down as chancellor but assumed the role as the university’s chief enrollment officer and an active friend-raiser and fundraiser in addition to being chancellor emeritus. The university is on an expansive track. Pullin led the process to devise its , “Lead On: Values in Action,” with aims to increase enrollment by about 5,000 students, creating an “unmatched” student-athlete experience and becoming a Carnegie R1 research institution. The plan has four foundational pillars: student-centered growth; research, scholarship and creative activities; athletics; and community engagement. An instrumental part of that plan is charting TCU’s academic path. “Daniel Pullin has been listening and preparing for his challenges as the ambitious plans for the continuing transformation of the university unfold,” said James Riddlesperger , a longtime political science professor who has taught American and Texas politics and is retiring this summer. “The four pillars of those plans are well underway, and Pullin’s major challenge will be to transform promise into reality.” The planned growth of the student body, along with difficulties of international students coming to America, Riddlesperger said, will “require forward-thinking leadership.” Rebecca Sharpless , who will serve as chair of the TCU Faculty Senate for the 2025-26 academic year, believes that Pullin has the ability to attain the goals outlined in the strategic plan. “As president, Daniel has shown himself to be concerned with and interested in the academic mission that is at the core of the university, and we anticipate that his interest will continue even as higher education faces significant challenges from chaotic national changes and the arms race of big-time athletics,” said Sharpless, a professor in American history. While president, Pullin prioritized innovation. He appointed a chief university strategy and innovation officer to cabinet level and supported a Name, Image and Likeness, also known as NIL, strategy in college athletics. “Chancellor Pullin is the right leader to take TCU into our next exciting chapter,” said TCU Board of Trustees Chair Kit Moncrief in a statement. “Daniel leans into TCU as a values-driven university and has an expansive vision for how TCU can be the model for student-centered success.” One of the ambitious goals outlined is becoming a top research university, or a Carnegie R1 research institution. In 2025, 187 institutions nationwide achieved that status. TCU’s rival to the east, Southern Methodist University, attained R1 status earlier this year. TCU’s plan is to get there with a 10-year runway. The Carnegie Foundation metrics to attain the status of “very high spending and doctorate production” involve $50 million in total research spending and graduating 70 research doctorates. TCU would need to double its research spending, which is about $25 million, and need to add about 30 more research Ph.D.s to get to that goal, said Pullin." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. subject: Re: Stop utility companies from charging YOU for their lobbyists and lawyers
body:
Action needed: write your lawmakers to support “getting junk out of our utility bills” TODAY.| | | Dear Peter, I'm writing to you today to ask for a last call of support for the passage of Get the Junk out of Rates **(**[**SB 88**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921842/549436910/1612794283?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230)**)**. It has been almost a month since there was a [public hearing on SB 88](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921843/549436911/-612806870?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230) and with only a few weeks left in session, the clock is ticking on this bill. We don’t have a lot of time left to Get the Junk Out of Rates by passing SB 88. [**Your legislators need to hear from you that you want to Get the Junk Out of Rates, so send your message now — even if you’ve already taken action on this bill!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921844/549436912/-1353810674?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230)

Dear Peter, As Oregonians struggle with skyrocketing utility bills, Oregon's largest utilities are raising rates, making profits, and passing unnecessary costs on to us. Our utility bills are paying for things like expensive lawyers, lobbyists, marketing, and industry association fees. That’s millions of dollars of “junk” on our utility bills every year. Tell your legislators to support Senate Bill 88 - Get the Junk Out of Rates which will help keep Oregonians’ utility bills low by holding large utility companies accountable. **[Can we count on you to contact your lawmakers in support of this bill?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921847/549436915/-1353810674?contactdata=8hLzOZBUFHFzNgAiYuqO4GpYxN5bhHPKic5//ZVWxO90lCsUdvivqtMetS1DbJ9GXxcwVE5stjSba5wM3HJy0wzCJmQ6E4TjZ1WvGKuEfWMNynReZv7xmYPNzX9hIK06&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230) **

---
**Oregon’s largest for-profit utilities have raised customers’ bills by about 50% in four years.** Cutting unnecessary charges from our utility bills is an easy way for state leaders to deliver relief to families struggling with rising costs. [Please make your voice heard, Peter, to get the junk out of our rates!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921849/549436917/-1353810674?contactdata=8hLzOZBUFHFzNgAiYuqO4GpYxN5bhHPKic5//ZVWxO90lCsUdvivqtMetS1DbJ9GXxcwVE5stjSba5wM3HJy0wzCJmQ6E4TjZ1WvGKuEfWMNynReZv7xmYPNzX9hIK06&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230)

---
We can protect our families and our budgets by transitioning to affordable clean energy, making energy efficiency upgrades, and holding large utility companies accountable. OLCV and the Building Resilience coalition are working to pass proposals that would help [keep utility bills manageable](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921851/549436919/-401923353?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230) for Oregonians. Oregonians need healthy, affordable homes that run on clean energy. We will get there by putting people over profit to ensure clean, affordable energy for all. Onward,
---
---
Eliza Walton _(she/they)_ OLCV Coalition Director _PS: If you want to learn more, follow[Building Resilience on Instagram](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108921852/549436920/474479323?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1YWRiMmQzLWMwNDAtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7HfyHlgenxiPEqoJoPjMTClThy8H3qJY1MDZr4HenD8=&emci=68f7c657-b740-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=65adb2d3-c040-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=2107230)!_

clean_body: Action needed: write your lawmakers to support “getting junk out of our utility bills” TODAY.| | | Dear Peter, I'm writing to you today to ask for a last call of support for the passage of Get the Junk out of Rates (). It has been almost a month since there was a and with only a few weeks left in session, the clock is ticking on this bill. We don’t have a lot of time left to Get the Junk Out of Rates by passing SB 88. Thank you for your support. Together, Eliza Walton (she/they) OLCV Coalition Director | Dear Peter, As Oregonians struggle with skyrocketing utility bills, Oregon's largest utilities are raising rates, making profits, and passing unnecessary costs on to us. Our utility bills are paying for things like expensive lawyers, lobbyists, marketing, and industry association fees. That’s millions of dollars of “junk” on our utility bills every year. Tell your legislators to support Senate Bill 88 - Get the Junk Out of Rates which will help keep Oregonians’ utility bills low by holding large utility companies accountable. Oregon’s largest for-profit utilities have raised customers’ bills by about 50% in four years. Cutting unnecessary charges from our utility bills is an easy way for state leaders to deliver relief to families struggling with rising costs. We can protect our families and our budgets by transitioning to affordable clean energy, making energy efficiency upgrades, and holding large utility companies accountable. OLCV and the Building Resilience coalition are working to pass proposals that would help for Oregonians. Oregonians need healthy, affordable homes that run on clean energy. We will get there by putting people over profit to ensure clean, affordable energy for all. Onward, Eliza Walton (she/they) OLCV Coalition Director PS: If you want to learn more, follow! | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Oregon League of Conservation Voters 1110 SE Alder Suite 220 Portland, OR 97214 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

Conservative commentator Scott Jennings **[schooled left wing journalist Tara Palmeri](https://substack.com/redirect/adeff25f-796a-4834-90ad-35dbfe683bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on the truth of recent violence coming predominately from the radical left.
"The left has a problem with violence, it is in its heart right now," Jennings told Palmeri.
[Watch The Video](https://substack.com/redirect/adeff25f-796a-4834-90ad-35dbfe683bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/adeff25f-796a-4834-90ad-35dbfe683bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"You can't just say its the left that is violent, we had an insurrection at the Capitol!" Palmeri argued.
"You had one bad day," Jennings fired back. "You have vandalism, you have Luigi Mangione worship, unbelievable, and the adherence to trying to keep these violent illegal aliens in the country!"
Palmeri claimed that there was violence on both sides of the political aisle, not just the left, "I don't think that's fair to say,... It's dangerous but it's happening on both sides!"
**["What person on the right is committing violence today?!"](https://substack.com/redirect/adeff25f-796a-4834-90ad-35dbfe683bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Jennings asked.
Watch Palmeri struggle to answer Jennings' simple question:
[Watch The Video](https://substack.com/redirect/adeff25f-796a-4834-90ad-35dbfe683bc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Elon Musk voices strong disapproval of Trump spending bill: _" Disgusting abomination"_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/64d7163a-3811-4b66-a521-480018532cdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Elon Musk escalated his criticism of a Trump-endorsed spending bill, **[calling it a "disgusting abomination"](https://substack.com/redirect/b5ff9626-a9dd-4479-a4bd-9c64bf1032b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and saying he "just can't stand it anymore."
* The outgoing DOGE chief argues the "massive, outrageous, pork-filled" legislation undermines all the cost-cutting work his team accomplished.

* Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Trump knows where Musk stands but "it doesn't change the president's opinion" - calling it "one big, beautiful bill" that Trump is "sticking to." The bill passed the House largely along party lines in May, though some Republicans like Rand Paul oppose it.
* Senate Majority Leader John Thune is trying to bridge the gap, hoping Musk "will come to a different conclusion" while explaining that the reconciliation bill only deals with mandatory spending, not the discretionary spending DOGE focused on. Thune says there are "ways to incorporate" the savings Musk found, suggesting they're looking for compromise despite the "difference of opinion."

# MAGA congressman SNAPS at Democrat Dan Goldman for interrupting him: _" You've spoken enough!!"_
GOP Rep. Bob Onder **[put Democrat Dan Goldman in his place](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after an attempted interruption while he was scolding Democrats for storming an I.C.E detention facility.
"You've spoken enough already!"
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Six Democrat politicians, including Newark's mayor, Ras Baraka, were arrested last month at the I.C.E detention center in Newark, New Jersey after they reportedly stormed the facility and attacked I.C.E officers. The Democrats claimed that they were protesting Trump's illegal immigration deportations.
In the hearing, Rep. Goldman of New York attempted to shame House Republicans for backing Trump and condemning the protesters.
"I know many of you think you take an oath to Donald Trump, but in reality the oath is to the Constitution," Goldman said. "Your own colleagues are being arrested for what is so obviously, obviously**[a politicized, trumped-up, bogus charge!](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** "
Onder then took the floor to question Scott Mechowski, retired ICE deputy director brought in to discuss Trump's deportation policies and the justification of I.C.E patrol against the trespassing officials.
"I do care whether illegal aliens have committed violent crimes, of course, but I think every illegal immigrant, as you've pointed out, Mr. Mechkowski, has broken the law, and needs to leave. And to me, due process is you prove that they're not here legally, then get the hell out of our country."
Goldman tried to interject.
"The gentleman yield for a friendly question -"
"No I do not! It's my time. You've spoken about four times so far in this hearing," Onder fired back, then hitting Democrats hard, "We members of Congress aren't above the law!"
**[Watch Onder's entire rant against the Dems here:](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/fa3a77f5-a89d-479e-ab3c-d041b657aa76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Jewish baseball champ SLAMS AOC for "virtue signaling" after Colorado terror attack
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5ad3458d-ac3b-4d7b-a8e9-de1de872985e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Kevin Youkilis, a Jewish former MLB star, **[called out Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez](https://substack.com/redirect/5ad3458d-ac3b-4d7b-a8e9-de1de872985e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for her response to the antisemitic terror attack in Boulder, Colorado that injured 12 people.
* **What AOC Said** : "My heart is with the victims and our Jewish communities across the country," **[the congresswoman said in a social media post.](https://substack.com/redirect/4db7d6e2-f261-4f46-a8e1-77d063c8c7ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** "Antisemitism is on the rise here at home, and we have a moral responsibility to confront and stop it everywhere it exists."

* **Youkilis ' Criticism**: The World Series champion accused AOC of "virtue signaling" in her response to the attack and called out her inaction against antisemitic rhetoric in her New York City district:
"Jews are targeted with violence and it's the same virtue signal post time and time again," the athlete wrote on X. "What have you done to confront those calling for intifadas in NYC? Until you create a plan of action, your repeated virtue signaling after the violence occurs holds no weight."
* **Details of the Boulder Attack** : The incident involved Mohamed Soliman, who allegedly used a homemade flamethrower and Molotov cocktails to attack Israeli protesters advocating for hostages in Gaza. Eight individuals, aged 52 to 88, were hospitalized. Soliman faces multiple federal charges, including hate crimes and attempted murder.
* **Federal Investigation Underway** : The FBI and Department of Homeland Security are treating the Boulder attack as a terrorist incident. Soliman, an Egyptian national who overstayed his visa, was arrested and is being held without bond.

Details of the Boulder Attack : The incident involved Mohamed Soliman, who allegedly used a homemade flamethrower and Molotov cocktails to attack Israeli protesters advocating for hostages in Gaza. Eight individuals, aged 52 to 88, were hospitalized. Soliman faces multiple federal charges, including hate crimes and attempted murder. Federal Investigation Underway : The FBI and Department of Homeland Security are treating the Boulder attack as a terrorist incident. Soliman, an Egyptian national who overstayed his visa, was arrested and is being held without bond. PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. _**AXIOS:**_ **Trump's 5-step push to keep GOP control of the House in '26**
* * *
Peter,

We’re just halfway through 2025, _and Donald Trump and his Republican operatives are already putting their full weight behind protecting and expanding their House majority in 2026._

→ They’re recruiting candidates to run in swing districts so they can flip blue seats.
→ They’re spending big on ads (already!) to shape the narrative in their favor so they can elect more extremists who will be a rubber stamp for Trump's agenda.
→ And Trump just headlined a fundraiser for the NRCC (the campaign arm tasked with flipping and defending GOP House seats) that raked in $35 MILLION in just one night.

**Kim’s seat is NOT “safe.”** And Republicans are already setting the stage for what will be a tough fight for control of the House.

But we’ve won tough races before, and we can do it again so long as this grassroots team is behind us. We can’t let our guard down—not for a second.

clean_body: AXIOS: Trump's 5-step push to keep GOP control of the House in '26 AXIOS: Trump's 5-step push to keep GOP control of the House in '26 Peter, We’re just halfway through 2025, and Donald Trump and his Republican operatives are already putting their full weight behind protecting and expanding their House majority in 2026. → They’re recruiting candidates to run in swing districts so they can flip blue seats. → They’re spending big on ads (already!) to shape the narrative in their favor so they can elect more extremists who will be a rubber stamp for Trump's agenda. → And Trump just headlined a fundraiser for the NRCC (the campaign arm tasked with flipping and defending GOP House seats) that raked in $35 MILLION in just one night. Kim’s seat is NOT “safe.” And Republicans are already setting the stage for what will be a tough fight for control of the House. But we’ve won tough races before, and we can do it again so long as this grassroots team is behind us. We can’t let our guard down—not for a second. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Kim Schrier for Congress Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. **Peter, foreign billionaires are using shady loopholes to FUND the Democrats’ radical agenda.**

While George Soros and the Left’s mega-donors bankroll candidates and campaigns, **leftists from around the world are pouring millions into Democrat initiatives — right here in America.**

_They’re rewriting our state laws, mandating abortion on-demand, boosting Democrat turnout, and opening up our elections_ — **all without breaking a single rule.**

_Unless we act now, this foreign-funded interference could destroy election integrity as we know it. That’s why Heritage Action is sounding the alarm and**calling on the Silent Majority to speak out.**_

name: Deb Haaland
subject: What I learned at Zinn’s Bakery
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
When I was 14, I started working at Zinn’s Bakery in Southeast Albuquerque.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)
When I was 14, I started working at Zinn’s Bakery in Southeast Albuquerque.
It was the first time I felt what it meant to be part of a small business. I still remember the smell of fresh bread and pastries in the early morning, and how Mr. Zinn took the time to teach me — not just how to decorate cakes, but how to serve people with care.
We woke up early.
We put care into each cake.
We learned kids’ birthdays.
We delivered on time.
We thanked our customers, and we meant it.
Years later, when I started my own salsa company, I did the same. I knew how to work hard. And there were times when I would _still_ barely scrape by.
On the campaign trail, I’ve met with small business owners and working families all across New Mexico who are doing everything right — and still struggling to hold it all together.
**I’m running for governor because it shouldn’t be this hard to open a business, pay the bills, or keep a roof over your head.** That’s why, as New Mexico’s next Governor, I will:
1. **Protect our small businesses from federal chaos.** When Trump and Republicans cut programs that small businesses count on, New Mexico will step up to keep our local economy strong.

2. **Double down on the progress New Mexico has made on site readiness.** We’ll build on recent wins to ensure businesses have sites that are move-in ready with utilities and infrastructure already in place.

3. **Bring new industries home.** From clean energy to tech to film, we’ll invest in sectors that create good-paying jobs and strengthen our small business economy.

This is a preview of my economic plan, and there’s more to come.
Building a future where every New Mexican can thrive takes all of us. This campaign isn’t backed by billionaires. It’s powered by people like you, who believe that hard work should be enough to live a good life.
**[If you’re in the position to do so, will you chip in $5 to grow our campaign and help us build a future where every New Mexican can succeed?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250603_1_zinn?amount=5&refcode=em20250603-2371&refcode2=2371_400544_rNuDl7&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250603_1_zinn?amount=5&refcode=em20250603-2371&refcode2=2371_400544_rNuDl7&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)**
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250603-1&t=2&refcode2=2371_400544_rNuDl7&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=2371%2E400544%2ErNuDl7)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)

# [Corporations Learn That Boycotts Work ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165125262&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTI1MjYyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODc1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU3OTU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XpQ6NoyFAq0UrwBnmDM1nnElq4BaKND4hY2QCL6typw)
### Target, Tesla, Law Firms, and The Washington Post are learning the hard way that bigotry is a bad business model
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jun 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165125262&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTI1MjYyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0ODk4NzU5OCwiZXhwIjoxNzUxNTc5NTk4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.EIOAsiL3Gvp05FUmW9Jhc4kx3hmjkgyP4bsf_WFoOvY&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165125262&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTI1MjYyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODc1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU3OTU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XpQ6NoyFAq0UrwBnmDM1nnElq4BaKND4hY2QCL6typw&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165125262&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTI1MjYyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODc1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU3OTU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XpQ6NoyFAq0UrwBnmDM1nnElq4BaKND4hY2QCL6typw)

---

Somewhere in a corporate boardroom, a CEO is crying into a $12,000 espresso machine wondering how it all went so wrong? Let me help them out: You bent the knee to Trump's bigotry. And now, you're paying for it. Turns out, when you slap the public across the face with fascism, they don't come back for more. Target, Tesla, large law firms, and The Washington Post are the latest faces of corporations who capitulated to bigotry and are now paying the price. How do we continue to pile on to ensure they heard our message? **Let 's Address This.**
**Support** _**Let 's Address This**_**as a paid subscriber and let 's continue to build this movement for justice together.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..oSY5OX5rAyLiFbUuFHQHvB67-awrvvuegtrbirGdvGc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=165125262)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8b7b11f2-5fba-4756-a6c1-ce0a79b6686a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Becky Dankowski holds an anti-Target sign during a news conference outside Target Corporation's HQ in Minneapolis. (AP Photo/Ellen Schmidt)
## **Target: The Bulls-Eye of Boycott Power**
In January Target announced it would phase out DEI initiatives, particularly those intended to ensure more meaningful representation of Black employees. In response, Georgia Pastor Rev. Jamal Bryant called for a 40-day-boycott of Target--one that he's since called to extend. And the American people responded.
The resulting record drop in foot traffic, revenue, and profits means Target is now seeing its worst performance in recent memory. By some estimates Target has lost $12.4B in valuation, with online sales and in store sales all trending down. All due to Target's cowardly retreat from diversity, which came after a few loud extremists got upset that Target dared to be inclusive. For those wondering, Costco is an excellent alternative to Target. While it isn't perfect, I give them credit for proudly leaning into meaningful diversity initiatives, and for paying their employees a much more livable wage of $22/hour.
## **Tesla: Fascism Doesn 't Sell EVs**
Then there's Tesla. Remember when it was the innovative darling of the future? Now it's the political equivalent of a gas-guzzling Hummer wrapped in a Confederate flag. After Elon Musk openly echoed Nazi talking points, praised Trump like a fanboy at a fascist fan convention, and made Twitter a playground for bigots, the world responded with the loudest answer possible: We stopped buying.
Global Tesla sales are collapsing. In some countries, sales dropped by as much as 49%. Musk somehow managed to turn electric vehicles into a right-wing culture war relic. That takes a special kind of talent.
## **Law Firms: The $100 Million Mistake**
As an attorney myself, it pained me to see law firms bend the knee to Donald Trump. Rather than stand up for justice and rule of law, several top law firms decided to throw their lot in with Trump, giving him over $100 million in pro bono legal services. Apparently, they missed the memo that helping a wannabe dictator dodge justice might not be the best look. As WSJ and Newsweek [report](https://substack.com/redirect/44882bd9-3bc9-4fc7-bac4-6426b9fe93ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> Legal executives from at least 11 major companies are redirecting assignments away from firms that made pacts with the White House, the WSJ reported. Oracle, Morgan Stanley, an unnamed airline, a pharmaceutical company, and [McDonald's](https://substack.com/redirect/cf0d54be-b41f-415b-8f47-2b432f42a16e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have all moved or considered moving legal work, in some cases explicitly citing dissatisfaction with the firms' response to White House pressure. [Microsoft](https://substack.com/redirect/79fc6752-c8cb-4fb5-bb8d-e6984b32708b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) raised conflict-of-interest concerns with Latham & Watkins, temporarily removing the firm from its preferred counsel list before reinstating it after further discussions.
And this doesn't even include the top lawyers who resigned from these so-called top firms, objecting to their capitulation to fascism. And moreover, it's important to recognize that this is a bipartisan failure. Even former first gentleman Doug Emhoff's firm capitulated to Trump, and [donated $100 million in free services](https://substack.com/redirect/6bd2834b-bbb0-4731-90d0-5ff4c60d6341?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Law firms are now paying the price as clients are fleeing to other firms who refused to succumb to Trump and his fascist ideals.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..oSY5OX5rAyLiFbUuFHQHvB67-awrvvuegtrbirGdvGc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165125262)
## **Washington Post: Democracy Dies in Sycophancy**
And finally, the Washington Post. Once a proud defender of truth, it has spent the past year gutting its newsroom and embracing billionaire-driven, Trump-friendly coverage. The reward for its editorial cowardice? It has reportedly lost more than 500,000 subscribers--that's reportedly more than 10% of its base.
Who knew that alienating your core readership in the name of billionaire appeasement wasn't a winning business model?
## **The Bigger Picture**
Let's be clear: This isn't just a series of unrelated corporate blunders. This is the power of organized, conscious resistance. This is what happens when people say, "Enough." Your dollar is your voice. And when you choose not to spend it on institutions that uphold bigotry and fascism, it sends a message louder than any tweet.
We are likely witnessing the most effective economic boycott since the Montgomery Bus Boycott. Target, Tesla, law firms, legacy media--all are feeling the consequences of aligning with hate and injustice.
So what do we do next?
**We keep the pressure on.** We double down on supporting local businesses, indie media, and writers who speak truth to power. We stop handing our money to corporations that treat representation and justice like optional accessories. Take stock of where you are spending your funds, and see how you can change your spending habits to support more local and small businesses and local writers and independent writers. Because this isn't just about economics. It's about integrity. And integrity, like democracy, only survives if we fight for it.
Boycotts work. Let's make sure corporate America feels the consequences of their actions.

clean_body: Target, Tesla, Law Firms, and The Washington Post are learning the hard way that bigotry is a bad business model | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription! Target, Tesla, Law Firms, and The Washington Post are learning the hard way that bigotry is a bad business model | | | Jun 3 | | | | | | | | | | | | Somewhere in a corporate boardroom, a CEO is crying into a $12,000 espresso machine wondering how it all went so wrong? Let me help them out: You bent the knee to Trump's bigotry. And now, you're paying for it. Turns out, when you slap the public across the face with fascism, they don't come back for more. Target, Tesla, large law firms, and The Washington Post are the latest faces of corporations who capitulated to bigotry and are now paying the price. How do we continue to pile on to ensure they heard our message? Let 's Address This. Support Let 's Address Thisas a paid subscriber and let 's continue to build this movement for justice together. | | ---|---|--- Becky Dankowski holds an anti-Target sign during a news conference outside Target Corporation's HQ in Minneapolis. (AP Photo/Ellen Schmidt) Target: The Bulls-Eye of Boycott Power In January Target announced it would phase out DEI initiatives, particularly those intended to ensure more meaningful representation of Black employees. In response, Georgia Pastor Rev. Jamal Bryant called for a 40-day-boycott of Target--one that he's since called to extend. And the American people responded. The resulting record drop in foot traffic, revenue, and profits means Target is now seeing its worst performance in recent memory. By some estimates Target has lost $12.4B in valuation, with online sales and in store sales all trending down. All due to Target's cowardly retreat from diversity, which came after a few loud extremists got upset that Target dared to be inclusive. For those wondering, Costco is an excellent alternative to Target. While it isn't perfect, I give them credit for proudly leaning into meaningful diversity initiatives, and for paying their employees a much more livable wage of $22/hour. Tesla: Fascism Doesn 't Sell EVs Then there's Tesla. Remember when it was the innovative darling of the future? Now it's the political equivalent of a gas-guzzling Hummer wrapped in a Confederate flag. After Elon Musk openly echoed Nazi talking points, praised Trump like a fanboy at a fascist fan convention, and made Twitter a playground for bigots, the world responded with the loudest answer possible: We stopped buying. Global Tesla sales are collapsing. In some countries, sales dropped by as much as 49%. Musk somehow managed to turn electric vehicles into a right-wing culture war relic. That takes a special kind of talent. Law Firms: The $100 Million Mistake As an attorney myself, it pained me to see law firms bend the knee to Donald Trump. Rather than stand up for justice and rule of law, several top law firms decided to throw their lot in with Trump, giving him over $100 million in pro bono legal services. Apparently, they missed the memo that helping a wannabe dictator dodge justice might not be the best look. As WSJ and Newsweek : Legal executives from at least 11 major companies are redirecting assignments away from firms that made pacts with the White House, the WSJ reported. Oracle, Morgan Stanley, an unnamed airline, a pharmaceutical company, and have all moved or considered moving legal work, in some cases explicitly citing dissatisfaction with the firms' response to White House pressure. raised conflict-of-interest concerns with Latham & Watkins, temporarily removing the firm from its preferred counsel list before reinstating it after further discussions. And this doesn't even include the top lawyers who resigned from these so-called top firms, objecting to their capitulation to fascism. And moreover, it's important to recognize that this is a bipartisan failure. Even former first gentleman Doug Emhoff's firm capitulated to Trump, and . Law firms are now paying the price as clients are fleeing to other firms who refused to succumb to Trump and his fascist ideals. Washington Post: Democracy Dies in Sycophancy And finally, the Washington Post. Once a proud defender of truth, it has spent the past year gutting its newsroom and embracing billionaire-driven, Trump-friendly coverage. The reward for its editorial cowardice? It has reportedly lost more than 500,000 subscribers--that's reportedly more than 10% of its base. Who knew that alienating your core readership in the name of billionaire appeasement wasn't a winning business model? The Bigger Picture Let's be clear: This isn't just a series of unrelated corporate blunders. This is the power of organized, conscious resistance. This is what happens when people say, "Enough." Your dollar is your voice. And when you choose not to spend it on institutions that uphold bigotry and fascism, it sends a message louder than any tweet. We are likely witnessing the most effective economic boycott since the Montgomery Bus Boycott. Target, Tesla, law firms, legacy media--all are feeling the consequences of aligning with hate and injustice. So what do we do next? We keep the pressure on. We double down on supporting local businesses, indie media, and writers who speak truth to power. We stop handing our money to corporations that treat representation and justice like optional accessories. Take stock of where you are spending your funds, and see how you can change your spending habits to support more local and small businesses and local writers and independent writers. Because this isn't just about economics. It's about integrity. And integrity, like democracy, only survives if we fight for it. Boycotts work. Let's make sure corporate America feels the consequences of their actions. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is made possible by you --the thoughtful person reading this. Join us & subscribe ❤️ | | ---|---|--- Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

Unpacking the Catalist Report
With Maria Cardona & Catalist CEO Michael Frias**
The Latino Vote Podcast Season 2025 Episode 15

With Mike Madrid off for the week, Chuck, Maria, and Michael dig deep into the latest **Catalist's 'What Happened in 2024' report** , detailing **Latino Voter trends since 2012** , with a distinct focus on the 2024 election. **From Obama’s 2012 10% share to today’s razor-thin margins** , Michael delivers the “Rocha Cliff Notes” on where Democrats gained, where they lost, and why the competition for Latino voters has never been fiercer.

Democrats are all in their opposition to President Trump's big, beautiful bill. The bill would eliminate the current 1.4 million illegal immigrants receiving Medicaid benefits at the expense of American taxpayers. Funny enough, Hillary Clinton agrees that illegal immigrants should not be receiving the same benefits that American citizens are entitled to. Dr. Sebastian Gorka is with me today to discuss the recent developments abroad as well as protecting the homeland. Ukraine unleashed 117 drones across Russian air bases, damaging or destroying 41 aircrafts, including bombers and surveillance planes. How will this development impede President Trump's ability to negotiate peace between the two countries? Dr. Gorka explains the depth and breadth of American military might and how we are a hyper-power, unrivaled by any country. The mainstream media is refusing to identify the anti-semitic terror recently unleashed in Colorado and Washington D.C. Two different men targeted Jewish people because of their faith and Trump's DOJ and FBI are calling them what they are, terrorists. Seb called out Politico at a town hall recently for protecting gang members and their anti-American rhetoric in a clip that has gone viral. We get his reaction to that clip and he goes FULL GORKA on Politico, again.

**Dr. Sebastian Gorka**

name: Randy Villegas
subject: This isn’t about me. It’s about all of us.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Villegas for Congress [#]
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP62/1/117525/images/Villegas%20Logo.png]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q2-2025?refcode=em-6.3.25-all-a [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q2-2025?refcode=em-6.3.25-all-a]

Friends, I’m so grateful.

Because of you, we hit our end-of-month fundraising goal. That’s more than a number – it’s proof that this campaign is built by teachers, students, farmworkers, nurses, and small business owners. Real people from across the country, coming together to build something better.

I grew up working at the swap meet every weekend, then at my family’s auto shop, just so we could make ends meet. That work ethic has never left me, and it’s at the heart of this campaign.

This is about families who’ve been left out and left behind, stepping up to demand the representation we deserve.

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q2-2025?refcode=em-6.3.25-all-a]

Meanwhile, my opponent David Valadao sides with extremists and billionaires, voting for a budget that guts healthcare and hurts the families he claims to represent – even though more than two-thirds of families here in Central Valley rely on MediCal.

We deserve better. And with your help, we can flip this seat, take back the House, and make sure working families – not corporate donors – are the ones shaping our future.

Thank you for being in this fight with me. Let’s keep pushing forward – together.

-Randy

P.S. The end of this month marks our FEC end-of-quarter fundraising deadline – our first since launching this campaign. All eyes will be on us, so it’s critical we show the strength of this grassroots movement. Will you chip in $3 today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q2-2025?refcode=em-6.3.25-all-a]

To support Randy's campaign consider making a grassroots contribution here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvc-web-2025?refcode=em-footer]

Donald Trump’s proposed military parade is more than just a spectacle, it's a wasteful, self-serving display that:

–Could cost nearly **$100 million in taxpayer dollars**
–Will **displace an annual event for Vietnam veterans** from the memorial built in their honor
–**Sets a dangerous precedent** that presidents can use public funds for undemocratic vanity projects

**This is not patriotism, this is ego at the expense of service.** Veterans should not be pushed aside so Trump can glorify himself.

clean_body: Trump’s military parade will displace Vietnam Veterans | | ADD YOUR NAME : | Donald Trump’s proposed military parade is more than just a spectacle, it's a wasteful, self-serving display that: –Could cost nearly $100 million in taxpayer dollars –Will displace an annual event for Vietnam veterans from the memorial built in their honor –Sets a dangerous precedent that presidents can use public funds for undemocratic vanity projects This is not patriotism, this is ego at the expense of service. Veterans should not be pushed aside so Trump can glorify himself. Thank you! name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Soon we’ll launch our Youth Fellowship program for 2025
body:
Together, we are empowering the next generation to fight for our shared future. | | |
---
Friend, Young people are critical to the future of our democracy. **That’s why we’re training the leaders of tomorrow, today.** On June 14th, we’ll launch our 2025 Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship where we’ll train young people over 6 weekends throughout the summer to learn from movement leaders and from each other. Fellows from 14 to 24 years old will build organizing and leadership skills through trainings and 30 hours of on-the-ground experience, after which they’ll receive a $1,000 stipend. **We normally select 15 fellows, but this year we received a record number of applicants (89!).** That means we need to raise closer to $20,000 to cover the costs of this important program, which is free to participants, so we can train more fellows than ever before. [**Will you help us expand the number of fellows we can accept into this year’s Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship with a donation today?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2E0qoB4Bqg7RkUJYOGNGAH_QNlsB_v451ZhoAFX1yloa7cvgDykNlgC1IYhh_PDpQxXRVRrG9ogqlKtHki7CWUJ5xlsp2rdwHhzj5iLti4cCxqm_QKyMoYXpXGjES-NCXvMB2tZ8_JicLYGyQEycGLMet4kkmI50bQWpQ5CCv7nhwYNjxXsAMY7Qwzm4i8nmEbTSXSiUqYmL3lkmRl6dwWk8o-8IxG1R07_92cn-v1ttm4V4A_t_7BGStX7_V-sjYEea1xUilA_lVM96vg5N_hVHFKhjBA6Ycc-d172UmxV169onHNNmlKOezLuHLLFpePH3OEe7I9VOWO6RRD7L3Cx6eOS0rZiNm80fnpv3Pzss/4h1/E2BbEJuLSdCI6XIpz_p69Q/h0/h001.5XxHwy77-zng_h9olaTUeDQlTk21VoV1RyqzqrxL-IU) | [DONATE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2E0qoB4Bqg7RkUJYOGNGAH8CikR6CV4uQuv81IrPBuedAz9J61abDXJedSpdS8bByZY3Pb0XMcb_phNLfK3IaAHwGzvZSVReria6_kLB1AXjAqta2AvPtFnWTxRup2m1yaWBtvrrzPRZJtafm4xfgo8A8HU99ttG-Nyw0GTK33d_OAdtBNj44KZFCSI2T_OevAjFxbIFuSBGJzlvn9LLbfuMLQxoAI165SAUwTKFjP1pdJTBdHrHRpP5vjaFJ724lO-6x7z8kKd2kekYWkk5hZ6DYGfCoygmFKo-BvfCans-PC7ofyUxitquLdjr44wf7qZp5-g7SfHebooXcxKah8lzi2URnVBG0lbVrlNjyK7G/4h1/E2BbEJuLSdCI6XIpz_p69Q/h1/h001.gM2IeZQuWzQh8oUgSTL79ebCeV_Vr7pMjcSa4L96kKI)
---
We’re also partnering with other Democratic members of Congress to train more young leaders about how to mobilize their communities for change. The Democracy Summer program educates high school and college-aged young people in the struggle for democratic freedom, including supporting them with hands-on experience registering and connecting with voters.
**Following a summer of youth civic engagement, we won’t stop there.** From August 2025 through May 2026, we’re giving young leaders a real seat at the table, offering a chance for high school students to work alongside me and other policymakers to create meaningful change through the Youth Advisory Council. They’ll develop initiatives to address key concerns in their communities and advise me about what they want from me as their Congressmember.
I’ve developed legislation in partnership with young people before, including my _Youth Homelessness Guaranteed Income Pilot Program Act_ to address youth homelessness by providing monthly cash assistance. Young people with lived experience helped craft the bill to ensure that it meets the real needs of our unhoused neighbors.
**Every time I get to meet with my district’s youth, their curiosity, thoughtfulness, and determination fuel my soul and remind me why we are fighting for an equitable society where everyone can thrive.** I just spoke at a local high school’s graduation, which filled me with pride and hope. Young people are our next generation of leaders, and we can help them build political power today.
[**Donate to my Rooted in Community Leadership PAC today to power our 2025 Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship and future youth engagement to train young people and grow the movement for a truly democratic society.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2E0qoB4Bqg7RkUJYOGNGAH9pusvBr_awfw9L8YOyhCrK4e3L4kryUNoNAMkkyH_HWjUdFzOVIOZOQjhJFszQLjuM8fwtCA3yz13b93cBua3RTrp0uSJ4tHr6D6tqGbYUqsZ9r6NxufOlhj8_7-Ouf_C8ROJW7Oqy_q97JPZCp3eH4BVnlibna0gfoYy6c5BKKAiLM-ckmmjUl-FnynP5rQfrv0nDO6I7YKx_1IdkZG7-AzE-AQYVWh2ojQRs6T2FG-UMVvAi2a_ee1a8psAfr-QIMDWwbBHJ1gyo6cwdlLymG4QlZQo0MU1seuW6qslT71lRlqIBk0FHdYFNe7KvGGlye-gDU75kdpM9Z-1NJ2zF/4h1/E2BbEJuLSdCI6XIpz_p69Q/h2/h001.e0IWOLH7gISE1Al7ENJyiKKqkWjA57WmltJfIP1sbN8)

name: Charlie Kirk
subject: Raise the Red Wall
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj2051jk5e4132js3kbgqoe81wb74mu97jwevw5trhkcs5r2i9rbmu2brbwh5lny4uskn1ube9wmo1f7igips&__stmp=sxawc7&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jek1z4xk2qpr49wfrblo8iicdr5nrtdm117xwyldfaix3yp9lghtsbu1bdvnzn0iyc6x1ergwyhoux9ncs6uk1sjl79lik7ltzce7cpkf7iq6q8d6imikxqabra5wvzbobvyvlgf2020fo64rhjr&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jek1z4xk2qpr49wfrblo8iid127dz9b4ekr19fpr01urjj37c2uzz8kvimz13irkuz8j2nhbzmbzgal6vxmqwzcaa42kfiulqqxz28qx5zjitnjk8fqvxx0kqok31p79al8lostej1dcn19rpwpn&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxawc7&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: Team Griswold
subject: Sign on for Equality this Pride month

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250603-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250603-email]
Friends,
This Pride Month, we’re celebrating the courage, joy, and resilience of the LGBTQ+ community—and recommitting ourselves to protecting equality and justice for all Coloradans.
From banning conversion therapy to expanding nondiscrimination protections, Colorado has made progress, but extremists across the country are working to roll those rights back. From issuing a ban on transgender individuals serving in the military, to cutting resources for LGBTQ Health Grants, the Trump Administration has taken discriminatory actions against the LGBTQ+ community.
We want to send a message this Pride Month: Colorado is a safe, welcoming, and inclusive state for everyone, regardless of who you love.
Can you sign our petition to show you stand for LGBTQ+ rights? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/raAabkx9s0q8KpXkTRCECw2]
Sign the Petition [https://secure.ngpvan.com/raAabkx9s0q8KpXkTRCECw2]Thanks for standing with us in the fight for equality. We’re proud to be in this movement with you.
— Team Griswold
Jena Griswold is running for Attorney General because Colorado needs a strong, proven leader in this critical moment. She'll stand up to Donald Trump to protect our rights and freedoms. She’ll fight for working- and middle-class Coloradans, hold big corporations accountable, and safeguard our land, air, and water.

#### This Memorial Day, while we honor the service and sacrifice of those who gave everything for our freedoms, we also reflect on what those freedoms are meant to protect—**honest government, fair opportunity, and a voice for the people**.
#### But in Juneau, the people are being forgotten.
#### The Legislature just closed its latest session—not with discipline, not with restraint, but with a **stampede of giveaways**. Millions of dollars in taxpayer funds handed over to the **National Education Association** and entrenched union bureaucracies—while schools close their doors, and more Alaskan families than ever turn to charter schools and homeschooling.
#### What was the dividend meant to do?
The **Permanent Fund Dividend** was designed as a promise—that all Alaskans would share in the wealth of our natural resources. It was never meant to become a **slush fund for lobbyists, consultants, and political machines controlled by out-of-state interests.**
#### This is not just bad budgeting.
It’s a betrayal of the people—and the purpose—of Alaska.
#### Our upcoming film, _The Permanent Short: Alaska’s Billion Dollar Betrayal_ , will expose this corruption. We’ll follow the money and show how generations of Alaskans are being denied their share, their voice, and their future.
#### And the movement is already growing.
#### Our **Angel tickets were the first to sell out for “Frozen Odds”** —a powerful signal that Alaskans are ready to fight back. Angel supporters receive exclusive access to both _The Permanent Short_ and _Frozen Odds_ , the film that lit the fuse.
#### This is just the beginning.
#### The fight for Alaska’s future isn’t over.
It’s just getting started.
---
#### [**Click Here To Get Your Angel Ticket**](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=683f7677dfdcfc630d25ac88&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tropictundra.com%2Fthe-permanent-short&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_683f7617d6a87004862a3e87&l=en-US&s=0jKvXb7EFcXBHTs_pmNBV--CB90%3D)
---
#### **In 2001, Enron collapsed under the weight of one of the largest corporate frauds in American history—over $25 billion evaporated overnight, leaving pensions gutted and lives destroyed. It was called the smartest disaster money could buy.**
#### But what if the next Enron wasn’t a corporation?
What if it was your own government?
#### This film asks the billion-dollar question:
**What happens when a state quietly reallocates tens of billions of dollars meant for its citizens—without a vote, without consent, and without accountability?**
#### We follow the money—through shadowy nonprofits, bloated bureaucracies, and politically connected networks like **Arabella Advisors** , **Alaska Venture Fund** , and the **1630 Fund**.
#### It’s a financial betrayal not buried in spreadsheets—but broadcast in silence.
**$25 billion gone** —not from shareholders this time, but from the people of Alaska.
#### This is _The Permanent Short_.
#### More announcements coming soon.
**Thank you for being part of this journey.**
---

### **Now we have 108 supporters for “Frozen Odds”**
---
#### **Frozen Odds How do you take on $15 million with nothing but determination, grassroots organizing, and a clipboard? Frozen Odds is a gripping documentary that uncovers the raw power of citizen-led initiatives in Alaska. Following the journey of a small but determined movement, this film reveals how everyday people—outspent 100 to 1 by out-of-state interests—rallied together to challenge the system. Led by an Alaskans for Alaskans who helped write the law and push it to the ballot, this battle wasn’t just about policy—it was about democracy in its purest form. With 400+ volunteers braving the cold, gathering 42,000 signatures when only 26,000 were needed, they proved that people—not money—should shape the laws that govern them. Through intimate interviews, on-the-ground footage, and stunning Alaskan landscapes, Frozen Odds captures the high-stakes struggle against political elites (aka Lisa Murkowski and George Soros, etc.) and the enduring spirit of grassroots activism. Though the campaign fell just 700 votes short out of 320,000, it laid the groundwork for a new wave of initiatives—this time, with three fresh petitions ready to take the fight forward. In a world where billionaires and lobbyists try to control the political process.**
#### **Frozen Odds asks a bold question: What happens when ordinary citizens refuse to back down?**

Frozen Odds is a gripping documentary that uncovers the raw power of citizen-led initiatives in Alaska. Following the journey of a small but determined movement, this film reveals how everyday people—outspent 100 to 1 by out-of-state interests—rallied together to challenge the system. Led by an Alaskans for Alaskans who helped write the law and push it to the ballot, this battle wasn’t just about policy—it was about democracy in its purest form. With 400+ volunteers braving the cold, gathering 42,000 signatures when only 26,000 were needed, they proved that people—not money—should shape the laws that govern them. Through intimate interviews, on-the-ground footage, and stunning Alaskan landscapes, Frozen Odds captures the high-stakes struggle against political elites (aka Lisa Murkowski and George Soros, etc.) and the enduring spirit of grassroots activism. Though the campaign fell just 700 votes short out of 320,000, it laid the groundwork for a new wave of initiatives—this time, with three fresh petitions ready to take the fight forward. In a world where billionaires and lobbyists try to control the political process. Frozen Odds asks a bold question: What happens when ordinary citizens refuse to back down? | | | | Follow Me On X @907Honest | | Phil Izon 12110 Business Blvd Ste A06 Box #113 Eagle River 99577 United States

subject: Was Moody’s U.S. Credit Downgrade Influenced By Their Anti-Trump CEO and Moody’s Commitments to DEI & ESG?
body:
Robert Fauber, the CEO of Moody's, has a long history of being a Trump hater.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..ofo73pGLoTFrZNfF5Z8EXeBLwNP1fsrzmyzuwp7cteg?) for more
---
[](https://substack..H50q2ekZE8CkbnwyfrWwrn3_YQ5yjiXVXiD83pJbyDA?)
---
# [Was Moody's U.S. Credit Downgrade Influenced By Their Anti-Trump CEO and Moody's Commitments to DEI & ESG?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1123743&post_id=164362629&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MzYyNjI5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODk5OTYsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU4MTk5NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMjM3NDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mKJtdJskBNGBllj0kC_PBRh8F_4mTqsl7FWB4SzYCGk)
### Robert Fauber, the CEO of Moody's, has a long history of being a Trump hater.
| | [Laura Loomer](https://substack.com/@lauraloomer)
---
| Jun 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lauraloomer)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1123743&post_id=164362629&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MzYyNjI5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0ODk4OTk5NiwiZXhwIjoxNzUxNTgxOTk2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTEyMzc0MyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sWkYRNtc9uvenIT9Ttq_M9aBzrHQDsJtQhWEACrjRk4&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1123743&post_id=164362629&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MzYyNjI5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDg5ODk5OTYsImV4cCI6MTc1MTU4MTk5NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMjM3NDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mKJtdJskBNGBllj0kC_PBRh8F_4mTqsl7FWB4SzYCGk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9e23e26c-9c06-4b9b-9f13-000faab2b515?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
